title: diospyros sandwicensis var. toppingii
intro: Diospyros sandwicensis var. toppingii, also known as Ohia lehua or simply Topping's ohia, is a variety of the Hawaiian tree Diospyros sandwicensis, a species of flowering plant in the family Ebenaceae. This plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, wounds, and inflammation. The health benefits of Diospyros sandwicensis var. toppingii include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments.
